title: bourreria havanensis var. radula
intro: Bourreria havanensis var. radula, also known as the Cuban shrub, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae. It is a type of shrub or small tree that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Caribbean and Central America, due to its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
